source: SCHEMA_MISMATCHES.md SM-003 (Phase 0 audit); reproduced in Phase 3 via testing/test_l2_content_search.py
title: L2 search filters on title only; content body keywords past char 100 unreachable
what_failed: memory_write(tier='l2') stores the full content under content.text (a JSONB field) but the title is only the first 100 chars of content. memory_read(tier='l2') filtered with ilike on `title` only. Distinctive keywords appearing past char 100 of the content were unreachable via L2 search — the chat-log production failure mode where Pi 'forgot' something it just stored is the same shape: write succeeds, recall returns 0 because the query doesn't hit the title bucket.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py memory_read() — the L2 branch did `builder.ilike('title', f'%{query}%')` only; no second query against content->>text JSONB.
why_likely: L2 was originally written for a structured-knowledge use case where a short title was the canonical handle. As L2 grew to hold richer content with the body in the JSONB field, the search path lagged.
reproduction: testing/test_l2_content_search.py — writes an L2 entry whose distinctive marker sits past char 100 of content (so it's NOT in title), then calls memory_read(query=MARKER, tier='l2'). Pre-fix: 0 results. Same with tier=None (which delegates to the L2 branch when L3 has no hit). Post-fix: 1 result, both via tier='l2' and tier=None.
expected: memory_read(tier='l2') matches against full content, not just title prefix.
actual: Pre-fix: title-only match. Post-fix: dual-query (title + content->>text) merged by id with dedup.
suggested_fix: Two queries: ilike on title and ilike on content->>text (PostgREST JSON-path operator). Confirmed working on supabase-py 2.28.3 via direct probe before applying. Merge by id, slice to limit. Empty-query behaviour preserved.

tests_passing: memory_read(tier='l2', query=MARKER) returns the entry whose marker is in body past char 100
memory_read(tier=None, query=MARKER) also returns the entry via the L2 branch
verification.probe_evidence: Pre-fix run: 0 results from both calls (entry confirmed in Supabase via direct .eq('id', ...) query). Post-fix run: 1 result from each. Dedup verified — the entry is not double-counted when title and content both happen to match.
verification.regression_check: testing/test_memory.py — 5/5 pre-existing tests still pass; testing/test_memory_tier_contract.py — 3/3 still pass; testing/test_session_id_propagation.py — 3/3 still pass.

scope_disclaimer: T-021 closes the L2 title-only filter gap. It does not address the broader memory-recall question (T-019, T-023): even with content search working, Claude's natural-language query formulation may still fail to hit the right keyword. That remains open.
